questions.question: A database that follows 2NF has to first meet 1NF.
1NF states that each table must have a primary key that is not a composite key.
One of the rules of 3NF is the database has no transitive functional dependencies. This\nmeans ________.
options: every child table has a foreign key that matches the size and datatype of the parent\ntable
changing a non-key column, might cause any of the other non-key columns to\nchange
the primary key is a numeric, auto-incremented key

questions.question: When using GROUP BY on a Primary Key you would ________.
options: expect a much smaller dataset then if you didn’t roll up the data
expect a dataset that is the exact same size as if you didn’t roll up the data
not have any expectations without analyzing the data in the tables
not be able to interpret the data

questions.question: When aggregating, the data will _______ come back sorted based on the grouped\ncolumns
To sort the Name field alphabetically from a-z and the Total field from largest to smallest\nthe ORDER BY clause will be as follows: __________.
options: ORDER BY Name ASC, Total ASC

questions.question: Because Indexes are used to speed up the execution of queries, DBAs will typically have\nmultiple indexes on the key column of parent tables.
The command to list all tables in a given database is _______.
Given a table with field value, to find if that value that matches the contents of a list you\nwould add the following to a WHERE clause: _________.
options: WHERE value IS GREATER THAN “A” and LESS THAN “C”

questions.question: Which of these is not a common use for an SQL trigger?
options: Complex auditing to track table changes

questions.question: How many records will the following query produce?\nSELECT a.movieId FROM Movie a INNER JOIN MovieActor b ON a.movieId =\nb.movieId
How many records will the following query produce?\nSELECT a.movieId FROM Movie a LEFT JOIN MovieActor b ON a.movieid =\nb.moveId

What would be the order of the actorId given the following query: SELECT DISTINCT
c.actorID FROM Movie a INNER JOIN MovieActor b ON a.movieId = b.movieId
INNER JOIN Actor c ON b.actorId = c. actorId WHERE genreId = 2 ORDER by name
ASC, dob DESC
Which query will produce a list of only the Movies that do not have any actors?
options: SELECT title FROM Movie a LEFT JOIN MovieActor b ON a.movieId =
b.moveId WHERE b.movieId IS NULL\nb. SELECT title FROM Movie a LEFT JOIN MovieActor b ON a.movieId =\nb.moveId WHERE a.movieId IS NULL
SELECT title FROM Actor a RIGHT JOIN MovieActor b ON a.movieId =\nb.movieId WHERE b.movieId IS NULL
SELECT title FROM Actor a RIGHT JOIN MovieActor b ON a.movieId =\nb.movieId WHERE a.movieId IS NULL

questions.question: It has been determined that Sally is also in the movie “size”. Which one of the following\nstatements adds Sally as an actor for the movie.
options: CREATE ROW IN MovieActor (movieId, actorId) VALUES (3, 6)

questions.question: What is the first normalization form that the Movie table fails?
The Actor table fails which database normalization form?
options: The Actor table does not fail any normalization forms

questions.question: Which query will produce the movie(s) with the oldest actors / actresses?
options: SELECT title, dob FROM Movie a INNER JOIN Actor b ON a.movieId =

b.movieId WHERE dob = MAX(dob)
b. SELECT title, dob FROM Movie a INNER JOIN MovieActor b ON a.movieId =
b.movieId INNER JOIN Actor c ON b.actorId = c.actorId WHERE dob =
MAX(dob)
SELECT title, dob FROM Movie a INNER JOIN MovieActor b ON a.movieId =\nb.movieId INNER JOIN Actor c ON b.actorId = c.actorId WHERE dob in\n(SELECT MAX(dob) FROM Actor)
SELECT title, dob FROM Movie a INNER JOIN MovieActor b ON a.movieId =\nb.movieId INNER JOIN Actor c ON b.actorId = c.actorId WHERE dob =\n(NOW()-dob)

questions.question: The Movie table is known as a ________ table because it only contains foreign keys that\nallow two other tables to join to each other.
Given two tables, table1 and table2, CROSS JOIN produces _________.
options: Every row where the key used in the join is in both tables
Every row where the key used in the join is in table1, regardless of whether or not\nit is in table2
Every row where the key used in the join is in table2, regardless of whether or not\nit is table 1
Every row from table1 joined with each row in table2

questions.question: Which would not be a reason to create a database view to present to your users?
options: Views always make accessing data more efficient
To protect specific data from specific users
They can replace tables to support legacy code

questions.question: The year field is an integer and we are only storing the years it takes to disintegrate.\nGiven a max number of years of 2999, what is the smallest data type to hold that value?
The description field is currently a varchar(150). How much space is taken up for this\nfield when the data in the field is only 50 bytes?
How would you add an index to the category table on the item field?
options: ADD INDEX index1 ON category item
ALTER TABLE category ADD INDEX item
USE category ADD INDEX index1 ON item

questions.question: How would you remove the category table?
A _______ is a named and optionally parameterized compiled set of SQL database access\nstatements that can be executed as a unit.
To combine the data from two separate queries one could use a ________.
Standard properties of a transaction are often referred to by the acronym ________.
What is the purpose of the “C” property, Consistency, in transaction processing?
options: Only valid data following all rules and constraints is written in the database
Enabled transactions to operated independently of each other
Ensures the result of a committed transaction is permanent in case of a system\nfailure
Ensures that all operations are completed successfully

questions.question: What is the purpose of the “A” property, Automicity, in transaction processing?
options: Only valid data following all rules and constraints is written in the database
Enabled transactions to operated independently of each other
Ensures the result of a committed transaction is permanent in case of a system\nfailure
Ensures that all operations are completed successfully

questions.question: Which of the following is not a commonly used column constraint in a database?
A primary key can allow duplicates in special circumstances?
When designing a child table, the foreign key column(s) in the child table must have the\nsame name as the primary key column(s) in the parent table.
When changing a column’s data type, which of the following is a true statement?
options: varchar(50) to char(20) may result in data loss
Int to tinyint will cause an error
Int to char will cause an error
Int to smallint will result in data loss

questions.question: A database will experience a deadlock when _________.
options: Two transactions try to update the same record
Transaction one locks table1 but needs table2; transaction two locks table2 but\nneeds table1
A database is being maintained while a transaction is trying to update a table
Transaction one is waiting on transaction two to be rolled back after a system\nfailure.

questions.question: SELECT id FROM testTable will return ________.
What will SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT fName), MAX(dob) FROM testTable return?
How many rows will the following query produce - SELECT 5*6+4 FROM testTable\nWHERE true?
How many rows will the following query produce: SELECT fName, count(id) FROM\ntestTable GROUP BY fName?
The following SQL statement - INSERT INTO testTable VALUES (24, “Ginny”,\n“Gerber”, “2000-04-04”) will have __________ as a result.
options: The table now has 6 rows, the auto-increment will take care of creating a new id.
The table will continue to have 5 rows and the row with id 24 will be overwritten
The table will have 6 rows, the id will be 24, different names assure uniqueness

questions.question: The following SQL statement - INSERT INTO testTable (lName, dob) VALUES\n(“Lincoln”, “2006-01-01”) will have ____________ as a result:
options: A new row will be added to the end of the table with a new id which is one larger\nthan the last id created, null first name, Lincoln last name, and dob of 2006-01-01
A new row will be added to the end of the table with a null id, null first name,\nLincoln last name, and dob of 2006-01-01
A new row will be inserted into the middle of the table with a new id which fills\nin with an unused id, Lincoln last name, and dob of 2006-01-01

questions.question: What will be the result set of the following query - SELECT fName, count(id) as idCount\nFROM testTable GROUP by fName HAVING idCount > 1?
What is the difference between the following two queries?\nSELECT fName FROM testTable WHERE id > 15\nSELECT fName FROM testTable HAVING id > 15
options: The two queries produce identical result
Having is only applies with a GROUP BY - second query produces no result
The query with the WHERE clause will error because id is not in the field list
The query with the HAVING clause will error because id in not in the field list

questions.question: When writing applications that use a database, it is customary to use the “root” user so\nthe application can accomplish the tasks it needs to accomplish.
In database terminology what is a sandbox?
options: The process of ensuring the database passes all Normalization forms
A development database separate from production
The name given to a multi-user database
The name given to a database backup

questions.question: What holds true for the following SQL to be valid - SELECT theName FROM\nfirstDB.table1 UNION SELECT theTitle FROM secondDB.table2?
options: theName must be a compatible data type with theTitle
theName must be the same data type as theTitle
Query can not run because column names must be the same
Query can not run because tables are from different databases

questions.question: Which of the following is not a standard key type on a database table?
Production databases automatically maintain referential integrity between tables using\nprimary and foreign keys.
Like a standard if statement, a WHERE clause evaluates in the order in which it is\npresented.
A duty a DBA is not typically in charge of would be ________.
